_description_listen_dns: IP:port for the plain DNS (UDP and TCP) listener. Must be an IP literal, never a hostname.
_description_listen_doh: IP:port for the local DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) listener. Must be an IP literal. A TLS certificate is auto-generated for this IP.
_description_listen_ui: IP:port for the web admin UI. Must be an IP literal. TLS is auto-enabled for non-loopback addresses when webui_force_tls_on_non_localhost is true.
_description_upstream_urls: HTTPS URLs of upstream DoH resolvers (e.g. https://9.9.9.9/dns-query). Must use IP literals. Order determines failover priority. If you use the template '{builtin:clientexe}'(without the single quotes, doh) it will be replaced with the querying executable name (useful for NextDNS URLs)
_description_upstream_sni_hostnames: TLS SNI hostnames corresponding to each upstream_urls entry (e.g. dns.quad9.net). Falls back to the URL host if omitted or shorter than upstream_urls.
_description_upstream_selection_mode: Strategy for querying upstreams: 'failover' (sticky, auto-heals), 'fastest' (race all, first valid wins), 'strict' (all must agree or query is dropped).
_description_upstream_retries_per_query: Additional retry attempts after the first try fails (0 = no retries; total tries = 1 + this value).
_description_block_mode: Action for blocked queries: 'nxdomain' (return NXDOMAIN), 'ip_block' (return block_ip/block_ipv6 addresses), 'drop' (send no reply).
_description_block_ip: IPv4 address returned for blocked A queries when block_mode is 'ip_block' (typically 0.0.0.0).
_description_block_ipv6: IPv6 address returned for blocked AAAA queries when block_mode is 'ip_block' (typically ::).
_description_qps_rate_globally: Maximum DNS queries per second across all clients combined (token-bucket sustained rate).
_description_qps_burst_globally: Maximum burst of DNS queries allowed globally above the sustained qps_rate_globally limit.
_description_qps_rate_per_client: Maximum DNS queries per second allowed from a single client IP.
_description_qps_burst_per_client: Maximum burst of DNS queries allowed from a single client IP above qps_rate_per_client.
_description_cache_min_ttl: Minimum TTL (seconds) for any cached DNS response, overriding lower upstream TTLs. Hard floor is 10s.
_description_cache_max_entries: Maximum DNS cache entries. New entries are silently dropped when the limit is reached until expired entries are evicted.
_description_whitelist_file: Path (relative to config.json) to the query-whitelist JSON file. Created automatically with an empty whitelist if absent.
_description_blacklist_file: Path (relative to config.json) to the response-IP blacklist JSON file. Created automatically with safe defaults if absent.
_description_hosts_file: Path (relative to config.json) to the local host-override JSON file. A domain must also match a whitelist rule before these overrides apply.
_description_log_queries: Path to the DNS query-only log file (JSON lines). Created automatically.
_description_log_file: Path to the full system log file (JSON lines, all levels including debug). Created automatically.
_description_console_log_level: Minimum log level printed to the console: 'debug', 'info', 'warn', or 'error'. File logs always receive all levels.
_description_log_max_size_mb: Maximum log file size in megabytes before rotation. Rotated files are renamed with a sequential numeric suffix (.1, .2, ...).
_description_allow_run_as_admin: If false (default), the process exits immediately when running with Windows administrator privileges as a safety guardrail.
_description_block_aaaa_as_empty_noerror: Return NOERROR with an empty answer for blocked AAAA queries instead of NXDOMAIN, preventing Windows from caching the domain as non-existent and breaking IPv4 fallback (e.g. ssh to github.com).
_description_allow_https_if_a_allowed: If true, an HTTPS-type DNS query is automatically allowed whenever an A-type whitelist rule permits the same domain, without needing a separate HTTPS rule.
_description_remove_https_ipv4_hints: Strip ipv4hint and ipv6hint parameters from HTTPS DNS records in upstream responses, forcing clients to resolve IPs via A/AAAA queries instead of using embedded hints.
_description_use_ede_in_blocked_reply: Attach an EDNS0 Extended DNS Error (EDE) record to blocked responses so clients and diagnostic tools can see a human-readable reason for the block.
_description_webui_password_hash: Bcrypt hash of the web admin UI password. Set via --hash-password flag or the WebUI config page. Never store a plaintext password here.
_description_webui_password_bcrypt_cost: Bcrypt cost factor used when hashing new passwords (minimum enforced: 12). Higher values are slower but more resistant to brute-force.
_description_webui_use_tls: Serve the web admin UI over HTTPS using the auto-generated self-signed certificate. Strongly recommended for any non-loopback address.
_description_webui_force_tls_on_non_loca: Automatically promote webui_use_tls to true when listen_ui is bound to a non-loopback address, preventing the password from being transmitted as plaintext.
_description_webui_max_login_failures: Number of consecutive failed WebUI login attempts from a single IP before that IP is locked out.
_description_webui_login_lockout_sec: Duration in seconds a client IP remains locked out after exceeding webui_max_login_failures.
_description_webui_read_header_timeout_s: Seconds the WebUI HTTP server waits for a client to send request headers before closing the connection (Slowloris defence).
_description_webui_read_timeout_sec: Seconds the WebUI HTTP server waits for a complete request body.
_description_webui_write_timeout_sec: Seconds the WebUI HTTP server waits while writing the HTTP response to a client.
_description_webui_idle_timeout_sec: Seconds the WebUI HTTP server keeps an idle keep-alive connection open. Auto-clamped to at least 2x webui_read_timeout_sec.
_description_max_concurrent_dns_tcp_conn: Maximum simultaneous DNS-over-TCP connections. Connections beyond this limit are rejected immediately to bound memory and goroutine count.
_description_max_concurrent_dns_udp_quer: Maximum DNS-over-UDP packets being processed concurrently. Excess packets are dropped rather than queued.
_description_client_tcp_timeout_sec: Per-operation timeout (seconds) for plain DNS TCP connections: reading the 2-byte length header, reading the body, and writing the response each receive this budget independently.
_description_max_recent_blocks: Maximum number of recently blocked domains tracked for the WebUI Blocks page (LRU eviction when full).
_description_local_doh_read_header_timeo: Seconds the local DoH HTTPS listener waits for a client to send HTTP request headers.
_description_local_doh_read_timeout_sec: Seconds the local DoH HTTPS listener waits for a complete HTTP request body.
_description_local_doh_write_timeout_sec: Seconds the local DoH HTTPS listener waits while writing the HTTP response.
_description_local_doh_idle_timeout_sec: Seconds the local DoH HTTPS listener keeps an idle keep-alive connection open. Auto-clamped to at least 2x local_doh_read_timeout_sec.
_description_cert_log_timeout_sec: Timeout (seconds) for the diagnostic TLS probe that logs certificate chain details when an upstream TLS handshake fails.
_description_upstream_dial_timeout_sec: Timeout (seconds) for establishing a new TCP connection to an upstream DoH server.
_description_upstream_client_timeout_sec: Overall timeout (seconds) for a single upstream DoH HTTP request including dial, headers, and body. Must be >= upstream_dial_timeout_sec.
_description_upstream_retry_backoff_ms: Milliseconds to wait between retry attempts to an upstream DoH server after a transient network failure.
_description_upstream_idle_conn_timeout_: Seconds to keep an idle upstream HTTP connection in the pool before closing it.
_description_upstream_max_idle_conns: Global maximum idle upstream HTTP connections kept in the pool across all upstream hosts combined.
_description_upstream_max_idle_conns_per: Maximum idle upstream HTTP connections per upstream host. Auto-clamped to not exceed upstream_max_idle_conns.
_description_doh_max_request_body_bytes: Maximum bytes accepted in an incoming DoH request body, guarding against memory exhaustion from oversized payloads.
_description_dns_udp_buffer_size: Per-packet receive buffer size in bytes for UDP DNS (512–65535). 4096 safely handles modern EDNS0 payloads.
_description_cachejanitor_interval_minut: Interval in minutes at which the DNS cache background janitor sweeps for and removes expired entries.
_description_cache_negative_ttl_sec: Seconds to cache SERVFAIL and other negative upstream responses, reducing retry storms during upstream outages.
_description_blocked_response_ttl_sec: TTL (seconds) embedded in DNS records returned for blocked queries, controlling how long clients cache the block response.
_description_localhosts_override_ttl_sec: TTL (seconds) embedded in DNS records synthesised from the local host-override file (hosts2ip.json).
_description_ui_log_max_lines: Maximum log lines shown per page in the WebUI log viewer. Older lines are omitted to prevent excessive RAM usage and browser freezes.
_description_extra_safety: Enable extra defensive checks: duplicate-entry detection in JSON files, power-loss staging files for atomic writes, and strict purging of malformed or duplicate rules on load. Recommended for production.
